---
title: "Results"
---

<!--
  This page is a template for you to fill in with your experimental findings.
  Add figures, tables, and commentary as you go.
  Quarto supports including Python/R code chunks that produce plots inline.
-->

## Training Curves

<!--
  Replace this section with your actual training plot.
  Example Python chunk:

```{python}
import torch
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

h = torch.load('../experiments/loss_history.pt', weights_only=False)
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3))
ax.plot(h['epochs'], h['train_losses'], label='Train')
ax.plot(h['epochs'], h['val_losses'],  label='Val')
ax.set_xlabel('Epoch')
ax.set_ylabel('MSE Loss')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
-->

_Add training/validation loss curves here._

---

## Conditional Mean Prediction (`coef 0`)

Results from `experiments/predictive_test_results_coef0.csv`.

<!--
  Example: heatmap of trained_relative_error over (theta, sequence_length)

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

df = pd.read_csv('../experiments/predictive_test_results_coef0.csv')
pivot = df.pivot_table(index='theta', columns='sequence_length',
                       values='trained_relative_error', aggfunc='mean')
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 5))
im = ax.imshow(pivot.values, aspect='auto', origin='lower',
               extent=[pivot.columns[0], pivot.columns[-1],
                       pivot.index[0],   pivot.index[-1]])
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label='Relative Error')
ax.set_xlabel('Sequence Length')
ax.set_ylabel('θ')
ax.set_title('Trained Model — Conditional Mean Relative Error')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
-->

_Add conditional mean prediction results here._

---

## Conditional Variance Prediction (`coef 1`)

Results from `experiments/predictive_test_results_coef1.csv`.

_Add conditional variance prediction results here._

---

## Bias–Variance Decomposition

_Discuss the relative contributions of squared bias and variance to the total error, and how they depend on $\theta$ and sequence length._

---

## Trained vs. Untrained Baseline

_Compare trained model error to the untrained baseline. Discuss what the improvement tells us about what was learned._

---

## Hermite Embedding Visualization

See `analysis_notebooks/embedding_vis.ipynb` for plots of the Hermite basis functions and their behavior across the trajectory values.

_Add representative embedding plots here._

---

## Attention Maps

_Add attention weight visualizations from `analysis_notebooks/analysis.ipynb`._

---

## Discussion

_Summarize key findings. Consider:_

- _Does prediction error decrease monotonically with sequence length?_
- _For which values of $\theta$ is the model most/least accurate?_
- _Does the model generalize to $\theta$ values outside the training distribution?_
- _What is the relationship between attention patterns and $\theta$ estimation?_
